title: Metrics Dictionary Completion Flow with Feedback and Modal Controls
story: As a DataTools Pro user completing a Metrics Dictionary analysis, I want structured completion controls (Start Over, Minimize, Complete) and a feedback prompt, so that I can signal when I am done, provide feedback on the Metric Analyst recommendations, and minimize the modal to check existing metrics without losing my progress.
description:
This enhancement overhauls the Metrics Dictionary modal workflow after the user runs the recommendation service. Once the second service call returns recommendations, the modal behavior changes:

### Modal Controls (replace the close button)
1. **Start Over** — Clears selections and returns to the first screen (dashboard picker)
2. **Minimize** — Closes the modal and shows a floating button in the bottom-right corner to re-open (maximize) it, preserving state
3. **Complete** — Triggers the feedback flow before closing

### Feedback Flow (on Complete)
- Displays thumbs up/down rating for the Metric Analyst
- Shows a text box: 'What additional instructions would you give to the Metric Analyst to prepare better recommendations?'
- Feedback is shown for both thumbs up and thumbs down
- Feedback is collected directly in the app database (not via Google Analytics)
- If no recommendations were added, the feedback form still appears

### Modal Behavior Changes
- After the recommendation service runs, the close (X) button and escape key are disabled
- Clicking outside the modal does not close it
- The back button is removed after the second service call (user must Start Over)
- Feedback appears every time metrics dictionary is run

### Analytics Intent
The product owner wants to track how many times a user clicks the magic wand, runs recommendations, and completes the feedback — to understand funnel drop-off and recommendation quality.
acceptance_criteria: After recommendations load, Start Over / Minimize / Complete buttons replace the close button
Start Over clears selections and returns to the dashboard picker screen
Minimize closes the modal and shows a floating 'maximize' button in the bottom-right corner
Clicking the maximize button re-opens the modal with preserved state
Complete triggers the feedback form with thumbs up/down and text input
Feedback is stored in the application database, not external analytics
Modal cannot be closed via escape key, clicking outside, or the X button after recommendations load
Back button is not available after the second service call
Completing the flow clears the selected dashboard for a fresh start on next use
ux_changes: Replaced the modal close button with Start Over / Minimize / Complete controls after recommendations load. Added a minimize/maximize floating button. Added feedback form with thumbs up/down and text input. Disabled escape and backdrop-click dismissal. Removed back button after second service call.
middleware_changes: Feedback submission (rating + text) is handled via application controller and stored in the database. Dashboard selection is cleared on completion.
data_model_changes: Feedback table or columns added to store thumbs up/down rating, text feedback, and user/session context for each metrics dictionary completion event.
implementation_notes: The product owner explicitly rejected Google Analytics in favor of first-party data collection. The minimize behavior mimics a chat widget (floating bottom-right button). A separate tracking table for funnel analytics (magic wand clicks, recommendation runs, completions) was discussed but implementation status is unclear — may be a follow-up.
comment_summary: Developer implemented Start Over, Minimize, and Complete buttons with the modal lockdown behavior. Developer asked about using Google Analytics for tracking; product owner rejected it in favor of direct database collection. Minimize was implemented as a bottom-right floating button. The need for a detailed funnel tracking table was discussed but may have been deferred.
